title: 10 best herbal creams for bone health

remedy_properties: Calcitonin: Some herbs like turmeric (Curcuma longa) contain compounds that mimic the action of calcitonin, a hormone that helps regulate calcium levels in the blood and promotes bone density.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Herbs like ginger (Zingiber officinale), ashwagandha (Withania somnifera), and ginseng (Panax spp.) have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation in joints and bones, which can contribute to osteoporosis and other bone-related disorders.

Antioxidant activity: Herbs like green tea (Camellia sinensis) and bilberry (Vaccinium myrtillus) contain antioxidants that may help protect bones from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with bone health because of its unique combination of flavonoids and terpenoids. These compounds have been shown to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation, which can help to promote bone repair and density. The antioxidants present in Ginkgo biloba creams also protect bones from oxidative stress, supporting overall bone health. Furthermore, Ginkgo biloba creams have been found to stimulate osteoblasts, the cells responsible for bone growth and development, leading to a stronger and more resilient skeletal system.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help with bone health by inhibiting the production of inflammatory cytokines, which can damage bone tissue and contribute to bone loss.

Terpenoids (specifically bilobalide): Bilobalide, a terpenoid found in Ginkgo biloba, has been shown to promote bone growth and density by stimulating the activity of osteoblasts, cells responsible for bone formation.

Quercetin (a type of flavonoid): Quercetin, a flavonoid present in Ginkgo biloba, has been found to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the body and promote bone health by inhibiting the activity of osteoclasts, cells that break down bone tissue.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ginkgo biloba are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help promote bone health.
Barks: The barks of Ginkgo biloba contain proanthocyanidins, which can help improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation, both essential for maintaining healthy bones.
Seeds: The seeds of Ginkgo biloba contain ginkgolides, which have anti-inflammatory properties and may help reduce pain and inflammation associated with bone-related conditions.
remedy_recipe: Melt 1 cup of beeswax in a double boiler at 180°f for 10 minutes.
Combine 2 tablespoons of ginkgo biloba extract with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and heat for 5 minutes.
Add 1/2 cup of shea butter and 1/4 cup of jojoba oil to the mixture and heat for 5 minutes.
Blend the mixture until it reaches 120°f and add 2 teaspoons of vitamin e oil.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool completely at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Frankincense cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as boswellic acids, help reduce inflammation in the joints and bones, which can alleviate pain and stiffness associated with conditions like osteoarthritis.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in frankincense cream, including terpenoids and flavonoids, help protect bone cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to bone degeneration and diseases like osteoporosis.

Analgesic and pain-relieving properties: Frankincense cream's analgesic compounds, such as incensole, help reduce pain and discomfort in the bones and joints, providing relief from conditions like fibromyalgia and arthritis-related pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Boswellia serrata, also known as frankincense, creams helps with bone health because it reduces inflammation and promotes healing in the body. The anti-inflammatory compounds present in Boswellia serrata cream, such as boswellic acids, help to alleviate pain and discomfort associated with bone disorders. Additionally, the cream's anti-oxidant properties may help to protect bones from oxidative damage and promote bone density, making it a potential natural remedy for conditions like osteoporosis and osteoarthritis. This can lead to improved bone health and reduced risk of fractures.
remedy_constituents: Acetyl-11-keto-β-boswellic acid (AKBA): AKBA, a triterpenoid compound, helps with bone health by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines that can contribute to bone degradation and osteoporosis.

α-boswellic acid: α-boswellic acid, another triterpenoid compound, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help in maintaining bone density and preventing bone loss associated with inflammation.

β-sitosterol: β-sitosterol, a phytosterol, has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help protect bone cells and tissues from damage, promoting bone health and density.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and promote bone health.
Rhyzomes: They are a good source of boswellic acids and other bioactive compounds that have been shown to improve bone density and reduce osteoclast activity.
Stems: The bark of Boswellia serrata stems contains boswellic acids and other compounds that have been traditionally used to treat bone-related disorders and promote bone health.
remedy_recipe: Combine 20 grams of boswellia serrata extract with 100 grams of a base cream containing 20 percent beeswax and 60 percent coconut oil.
Heat the mixture to 180 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes to ensure the ingredients are well combined.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to 120 degrees fahrenheit within 15 minutes.
Add 50 grams of a natural emulsifier and 10 grams of a preservative to the mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into containers and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes before use.

remedy_properties: Curcuminoids: These powerful anti-inflammatory compounds have been shown to reduce osteoclast activity, which helps prevent excessive bone resorption and promotes bone density.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with bone health because of its high content of curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help reduce inflammation in the body, which is a major contributor to bone degeneration. By decreasing inflammation, curcuminoids promote the production of new bone tissue and strengthen existing bone cells. Additionally, curcumin has been shown to enhance bone density and improve bone mineralization, making it an effective natural remedy for maintaining strong and healthy bones.
remedy_constituents: Curcuminoids: Specifically, curcumin, a major bioactive compound in Curcuma longa, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, promoting bone health by reducing the risk of osteoporosis and fractures.

Polysaccharides: Polysaccharides, such as turmeric-derived arabinogalactan, have prebiotic properties that help stimulate the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, supporting the immune system and promoting bone health by enhancing calcium absorption and bone mineralization.

Volatile oils (sesquiterpenoids): Volatile oils in Curcuma longa, such as alpha-curcumene and beta-curcumene, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, promoting bone health by reducing the risk of bone resorption and osteoporosis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of Curcuma longa contain the highest concentration of curcumin, a bioactive compound that reduces inflammation and promotes bone health.
Leaves: Leaves: The leaves of Curcuma longa are rich in antioxidants and contain curcuminoids, which help protect bones from oxidative stress and damage.
Roots: Roots: The roots of Curcuma longa contain a significant amount of curcumin and other bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and promote bone density.
remedy_recipe: Grate 500g of fresh curcuma longa tubers using a food grater for 5 minutes.
Mix the grated curcuma longa with 200ml of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally to avoid burning.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container to remove solids for 10 minutes.
Add 10ml of beeswax to the mixture and stir until it's fully melted and cooled to room temperature.

remedy_properties: Anabolic Properties: Ashwagandha creams can stimulate anabolism, which helps to promote bone growth and density by increasing the production of osteoblasts (cells responsible for bone formation).

Anti-Inflammatory Activity: Ashwagandha's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation in the body, which is a major risk factor for bone loss and osteoporosis. By reducing inflammation, ashwagandha creams can help protect against bone resorption.

Antioxidant Activity: Ashwagandha's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can damage bones and contribute to age-related bone loss. By protecting against oxidative stress, ashwagandha creams can help maintain healthy bones.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Withania somnifera, also known as ashwagandha, creams helps with bone health because of its rich antioxidant properties and ability to promote bone mineralization. The herb's adaptogenic properties help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, creating an environment conducive to bone growth and density. Additionally, ashwagandha creams may increase osteoblast activity, the cells responsible for bone formation, and reduce osteoclast activity, the cells that break down bone tissue. This results in stronger, healthier bones and potentially reduces the risk of osteoporosis and fractures.
remedy_constituents: Withanolides: These alkaloid compounds help with bone health by promoting osteoblast activity, which is essential for bone formation and repair, and also by reducing inflammation that can lead to bone loss.

Withaferin A: This withanolide acts as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, which helps to protect bones from oxidative stress and inflammation that can cause bone degradation and osteoporosis.
Withanoside IV: This saponin glycoside has been shown to promote osteoblast differentiation and bone mineralization, ultimately leading to improved bone health and density.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Withania somnifera are rich in alkaloids like withanolides, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help promote bone health.
Leaves: The leaves of Withania somnifera contain withanolides and other compounds that may help to reduce inflammation and improve bone density.
Seeds: The seeds of Withania somnifera are a good source of withanolides and other bioactive compounds that may help to protect bone cells and promote bone health.
remedy_recipe: Extract 30 grams of dried withania somnifera root in 1 liter of boiling water for 15-20 minutes.
Filter the mixture through a cheesecloth to separate the liquid from the solids within 30 minutes.
Combine the filtered liquid with 100 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture and stir constantly until the beeswax and coconut oil are fully incorporated within 10-15 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a sterilized container and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 2 hours.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Ginseng creams contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can damage bones and contribute to conditions like osteoporosis.
Bone density enhancement: Some studies suggest that ginseng extracts may help increase bone density by stimulating the production of osteoblasts, the cells responsible for building new bone tissue.

Inflammation reduction: Ginseng creams may contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in joints and bones, which can alleviate symptoms of conditions like arthritis and osteoarthritis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng, also known as ginseng, creams helps with bone health because of its ability to stimulate osteoblasts, the cells responsible for bone formation. The cream's active compounds, such as ginsenosides, may also enhance bone density by increasing the expression of genes involved in bone growth and development. Additionally, ginseng creams may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to bone loss and osteoporosis. This may lead to improved bone mineral density and reduced risk of fractures.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These triterpenoid saponins help with bone health by stimulating osteoblasts, the cells responsible for bone formation, and inhibiting the activity of osteoclasts, which break down bone tissue.

Saponins: Specifically, the saponins in Panax ginseng, such as ginsenoside Rb1, help with bone health by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in bone tissue, creating an environment conducive to bone growth and density.

Friedelin: This triterpenoid is involved in the regulation of osteoblast and osteoclast activity, and helps with bone health by modulating the expression of genes involved in bone metabolism and promoting bone formation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Panax ginseng are primarily used due to their high concentration of ginsenosides, which are believed to promote bone health.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are also used due to their high content of ginsenosides, which are thought to have anti-inflammatory properties that can benefit bone health.
Leaves: Leaves are sometimes used because they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may help to protect and support bone health.
remedy_recipe: Extract 3 grams of panax ginseng roots using 2 liters of 70% ethanol for 2 hours.
Filter the liquid through a buchner funnel to obtain a clear liquid extract.
Melt 200 grams of candelilla wax in a double boiler at 160°f to 180°f temperature.
Mix the liquid extract with 200 grams of shea butter and 100 grams of aloe vera gel.
Stir the mixture and pour it into jars before allowing it to cool and set.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Nettle cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like beta-sitosterol, which reduce inflammation in joints and bones, helping to alleviate conditions like osteoarthritis and improving joint mobility.

Mineral-rich: Nettle leaves are rich in minerals like calcium, magnesium, and silica, which are essential for maintaining strong bones and preventing conditions like osteoporosis by promoting bone density and mineralization.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in nettle cream, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to neutralize free radicals that can damage bone cells and tissues, leading to conditions like osteopenia and osteoporosis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica, also known as stinging nettle, creams helps with bone health because they are rich in silicic acid, a naturally occurring compound that supports collagen production and bone mineralization. The silicic acid in stinging nettle creams may also enhance calcium absorption and reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to the development of osteoporosis. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of stinging nettle may help alleviate joint pain and promote overall bone health, making it a popular natural remedy for osteoporosis prevention.
remedy_constituents: Alkylamides: These compounds have been shown to stimulate osteoblasts, the cells responsible for bone formation, and inhibit osteoclasts, the cells that break down bone tissue, thereby promoting bone health and density.

Fatty acids: Specifically, the omega-3 fatty acids present in Urtica dioica cream, such as alpha-linolenic acid (ALA), have been found to reduce inflammation and promote bone regeneration, which can help alleviate bone-related disorders.

Phenolic acids (e.g., rosmarinic acid): These compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, contributing to improved bone health and reduced risk of bone-related diseases.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals, which help to promote bone health by reducing inflammation and improving collagen production.
Roots: They contain a high concentration of mucilage, which helps to soothe and nourish the skin, promoting bone health by reducing inflammation and improving skin elasticity.
Stems: They are rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with bone health issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/2 cup of fresh urtica dioica leaves in late spring or early summer when they are at their peak potency.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer on paper towels for 1-2 hours or until they reach a brittle consistency.
Infuse 2 tablespoons of dried urtica dioica leaves in 2 cups of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to create a decoction.
Strain the infused decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bowl and discard the solids.
Mix the strained decoction with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/2 cup of shea butter in a double boiler until the mixture is smooth and creamy.

remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Red clover creams contain isoflavones, which have been shown to stimulate osteoblasts (bone-forming cells) and inhibit osteoclasts (bone-resorbing cells), promoting healthy bone density and strength.

Phytoestrogens: Phytoestrogens in red clover cream mimic the effects of estrogen in the body, which helps maintain bone density by stimulating bone growth and reducing bone loss, particularly in postmenopausal women.

Antioxidants: Antioxidants present in red clover cream help protect bones from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to osteoporosis and other bone-related disorders by damaging bone tissue and impairing calcium absorption.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Trifolium pratense, also known as red clover, creams helps with bone health because they are rich in isoflavones, specifically genistein and formononetin. These isoflavones have been shown to promote bone density by stimulating the production of osteoblasts, the cells responsible for bone growth and repair. Additionally, red clover creams may help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to bone loss and other age-related health issues, ultimately supporting overall bone health and well-being. This makes it a popular natural remedy for bone health.
remedy_constituents: Isoflavones: Isoflavones, particularly genistein and daidzein, have been shown to promote bone health by inhibiting the activity of osteoclasts, which are cells responsible for bone resorption.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help protect bone cells from oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting bone health.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, including naringenin and fisetin, have been found to stimulate the activity of osteoblasts, which are cells responsible for bone formation, thereby promoting bone growth and density.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in isoflavones, particularly formononetin and genistein, which may help alleviate menopausal symptoms and promote bone health.
Roots: Contain coumarins and flavonoids, which may have a potential role in bone health by modulating inflammatory responses and improving bone mineral density.
Seeds: High in isoflavones, triterpenoids, and other bioactive compounds, which may contribute to their potential bone-protective and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest trifolium pratense flowers at full bloom and dry them in a single layer at room temperature for 7-10 days.
Steep 20g of dried trifolium pratense flowers in 100ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the oil mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids then transfer it to a clean container.
Mix the infused oil with beeswax in a ratio of 1:1 by weight to create a smooth paste.
Heat the mixture gently in a double boiler for 10-15 minutes or until the beeswax is fully melted.

remedy_properties: Silica content: Horsetail cream contains high amounts of silica, which helps to improve bone density by increasing calcium absorption and reducing calcium excretion in urine, leading to stronger bones.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Horsetail cream has anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce inflammation in the body, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with conditions such as arthritis, osteoporosis, and bone fractures, promoting healthier joints and bones.

Antioxidant properties: Horsetail cream contains antioxidants that protect bones from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, helping to maintain healthy bone tissue and prevent age-related bone loss.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Equisetum arvense, also known as field horsetail, creams helps with bone health because it is rich in silica, a mineral that plays a crucial role in bone formation and density. Silica helps to strengthen bones, improve bone mineralization, and reduce the risk of fractures. Additionally, field horsetail creams may help to improve bone flexibility and reduce inflammation, making it a potential natural remedy for bone-related issues such as osteoporosis and arthritis. Its anti-inflammatory properties also contribute to its bone health benefits.
remedy_constituents: Silica: Helps with bone health by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving bone density, and enhancing calcium absorption, ultimately promoting bone strength and reducing the risk of fractures.

Alkaloids (specifically, equisetic acid and equistatin): Contribute to bone health by inhibiting the activity of osteoclasts, which are cells that break down bone tissue, thus reducing bone resorption and promoting bone preservation.

Triterpenoids (specifically, equisetonin): Exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the joints and bones, alleviating pain and discomfort associated with bone-related disorders.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in silica and other minerals, which are believed to support bone health.
Stems: Contain a high concentration of silica, which can help strengthen bones and improve bone density.
Leaves: Contain silica, flavonoids, and other compounds that may help promote bone health and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 bunches of equisetum arvense in the early morning or late evening when the moisture is highest.
Dry the harvested equisetum arvense in a single layer at room temperature for 1-2 weeks or until brittle.
Steep 2-3 tablespoons of dried equisetum arvense in 1 cup of hot water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the equisetum arvense infusion through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Mix 2-3 tablespoons of the equisetum arvense infusion with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil to create the cream.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps to stimulate collagen synthesis, which can improve bone density and reduce the risk of osteoporosis by promoting healthy bone growth.
Licoric acid: Has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the body, which is a major contributing factor to bone loss and osteoporosis.
Flavonoids: Act as antioxidants, scavenging free radicals that can damage bones and contribute to conditions like osteopenia and osteoporosis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with bone health because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body. These creams may also help to increase bone density by regulating the activity of osteoblasts, cells responsible for bone formation, and inhibiting the activity of osteoclasts, cells that break down bone tissue. Additionally, licorice creams may help to balance hormone levels, which is essential for maintaining healthy bones.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin helps with bone health by inhibiting the activity of osteoclasts, cells responsible for bone breakdown, thereby promoting bone formation and density.

Licoricidin: This phenolic compound has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation in bones and joints, promoting healing and reducing the risk of bone-related disorders.
Liquiritigenin: This phenolic compound has been reported to have antioxidant properties, which may help protect bones from oxidative stress and damage, promoting overall bone health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Glycyrrhizin, a compound found in licorice root, has anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate bone pain and reduce inflammation.
Roots: Glycyrrhizin also helps in regulating cortisol levels, which can contribute to bone health by reducing the breakdown of bone tissue.
Roots: Glycyrrhizin has been shown to inhibit the activity of certain enzymes that contribute to bone resorption, thereby promoting bone health.
remedy_recipe: Combine 150g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots with 250ml of boiling water and let steep for 15 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the solids then add 250ml of cold water and stir well.
Mix 100g of beeswax with 100g of shea butter in a double boiler and melt until smooth.
Gradually add the herbal infusion to the melted wax mixture and stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a container and let cool and set for at least 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Vitamin K: Dandelion creams contain vitamin K, which plays a crucial role in calcium metabolism and bone mineralization, helping to maintain strong bones and preventing osteoporosis.

Fatty Acids: The fatty acids present in dandelion creams have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation associated with bone diseases such as arthritis and osteoarthritis, promoting joint health and bone density.

Antioxidants: Dandelion creams are rich in antioxidants, including flavonoids and terpenes, which help protect bones from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of bone disorders like osteoporosis and fractures.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, creams helps with bone health because it contains high levels of calcium and other minerals essential for bone development and density. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also reduce joint pain and swelling, making it easier for bones to absorb and utilize calcium. Additionally, the cream's antioxidant properties help protect bones from oxidative stress, promoting overall bone health and reducing the risk of osteoporosis and related conditions. This natural remedy supports bone strength and density.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Taraxacum officinale creams may help promote bone health by inhibiting the activity of osteoclasts, which are cells responsible for bone breakdown and resorption.
Saponins: Saponins in Taraxacum officinale creams may help support bone health by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to bone loss and osteoporosis.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in Taraxacum officinale creams may help promote bone health by reducing the activity of osteoclasts and increasing the activity of osteoblasts, which are cells responsible for bone formation.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used in creams for bone health due to their high content of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Leaves: Leaves are used in creams for bone health due to their rich source of calcium, which is essential for bone mineralization and density.
Roots: Roots are used in creams for bone health due to their high content of saponins, which have been shown to promote bone mineralization and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale flowers and leaves in the early morning after dew has fallen.
Dry the harvested taraxacum officinale flowers and leaves in a warm dark place for 2 weeks.
Infuse 200 grams of dried taraxacum officinale flowers and leaves in 1 liter of carrier oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids then mix with beeswax to create a 20% concentration.
Whip the mixture with a hand mixer until it reaches a smooth creamy consistency taking about 5 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Borage creams helps with bone health because of its rich content of gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), an omega-6 fatty acid that has potent anti-inflammatory properties. GLA works to reduce inflammation in joints and bones, which can help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as osteoarthritis and rheumatoid arthritis. Additionally, the antioxidants present in borage creams help protect bone cells from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy bone density and reducing the risk of fractures.
remedy_properties: Glycosides: Borage cream contains glycosides, which have been shown to stimulate collagen production and improve skin elasticity, helping to promote healthy bones by supporting joint and connective tissue structure.

Omega-6 fatty acids: Herbal borage creams rich in omega-6 fatty acids, particularly gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce bone resorption and promote bone growth and density.

Vitamins and minerals: Borage cream often contains a blend of vitamins (such as vitamin E) and minerals (like calcium, magnesium, and potassium) that are essential for maintaining strong bones by supporting calcium absorption, bone mineralization, and bone density.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce bone inflammation and promote bone healing.
Flowers: High in gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), an omega-6 fatty acid that helps to regulate calcium metabolism and improve bone density.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh borago officinalis leaves and flowers, ensure cleanliness and dryness.
Combine 20 grams of dried borago officinalis mixture with 100 ml of carrier oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at low temperature for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, to extract herbal properties.
Filter the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discard solids.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax with 50 ml of the filtered oil and heat gently until melted.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with bone health because they contain powerful anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce joint pain and stiffness, allowing for greater mobility and flexibility. The cream's antioxidants also help to repair and protect bone tissue, making it an effective natural remedy for conditions such as osteoarthritis and rheumatoid arthritis. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to keep skin hydrated and healthy, which is essential for maintaining strong bones.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Pot marigold cream's antioxidant properties help protect bones from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to osteoporosis and other bone disorders.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in pot marigold cream reduce inflammation in the body, which is a key factor in many bone-related diseases such as arthritis, osteoarthritis, and osteopenia.

Calcium-binding properties: Pot marigold's calcium-binding capacity helps to retain calcium ions in bones, promoting healthy bone density and structure, and potentially reducing the risk of osteoporosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and promoting bone healing.
Seeds: High in calcium and other minerals essential for bone growth and development, adding to their benefits as a natural remedy for bone health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers and dry them thoroughly using a food dehydrator or air drying method.
Combine 200g of dried calendula flowers with 1000ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 6 weeks shaking the jar daily to ensure even extraction of plant compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container discarding the solids and reserving the liquid extract.
Mix the calendula extract with beeswax and vitamin d3 oil in a 2:1 ratio and melt the blend using a double boiler for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with bone health because it contains high levels of calcium, magnesium, and phosphorus that are essential for maintaining strong bones. Additionally, sage has been shown to increase bone density by stimulating osteoblasts, which are cells responsible for building new bone tissue. This can help alleviate symptoms of osteoporosis, such as brittle or fragile bones, and reduce the risk of fractures. By applying sage creams topically, it allows these beneficial compounds to be absorbed directly into the skin, providing targeted support for bone health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal sage creams contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to osteoporosis and bone loss.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage cream's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce bone resorption (the breakdown of bone tissue) by inhibiting enzymes that break down bones, promoting healthy bone density.
Calcium-absorbing properties: Herbal sage creams contain compounds that enhance calcium absorption in the gut, which is essential for maintaining strong and healthy bones.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots have been traditionally used to treat arthritis and joint pain, making them an excellent addition to medicinal creams for bone health.
Barks: The bark of the sage plant has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in joints and bones, promoting healthy bone development.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh salvia officinalis leaves and chop them finely into small pieces.
Steep the chopped leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 30 minutes to extract their bioactive compounds.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and discard the solids, retaining the infused liquid.
Mix the infused liquid with 12 ounces of coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of beeswax, heating the mixture gently over low heat until the wax melts.
Remove from heat, let cool and whip into a creamy consistency before transferring to an airtight container for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with bone health because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that work to strengthen bones and reduce the risk of osteoporosis. The thymol in thyme has been shown to stimulate collagen production, which is essential for healthy bone tissue, while the calcium content helps to fortify bones and prevent fractures. Regular use of thyme creams may also promote bone density and improve overall skeletal health.
remedy_properties: Thymol: Thymol, a compound found in thyme, has antimicrobial properties that help reduce inflammation and prevent infection, which can aid in promoting healthy bones by reducing the risk of bone diseases such as osteomyelitis.

Thujone: Thujone, another compound present in thyme, has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with bone disorders, such as arthritis, and promote bone healing.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid, a polyphenol found in thyme, has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in bones, which can contribute to conditions like osteoporosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation and promote bone healing.
Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of thyme plants (rhyzomes) have been shown to possess antioxidant properties that aid in bone density maintenance.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers then dry them immediately using shade or low-temperature drying methods within 2 hours.
Combine 20 grams of dried thymus vulgaris herbs with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture at room temperature for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily, to allow for decarboxylation and infusion processes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil which should take about 10 minutes.

Mix 100 milliliters of beeswax with 200 milliliters of the infused thymus vulgaris oil in a heat-resistant glass bowl over low heat using a double boiler method for approximately 30 minutes until smooth and consistent.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oats creams helps with bone health because of its unique composition, rich in antioxidants and beta-glucans. These compounds help to reduce inflammation in the body, which is a major contributor to osteoporosis and bone loss. The beta-glucans also stimulate the production of bone-building cells, promoting healthy bone density and strength. Additionally, oats creams contain silicon, a mineral that plays a crucial role in collagen synthesis, further supporting bone health and reducing the risk of fractures.
remedy_properties: Saponins: Saponins, found in oat plants, have been shown to inhibit the production of inflammatory cytokines, which can contribute to bone loss and osteoporosis. This property helps reduce inflammation and promotes bone health.

Avenanthramides: Avenanthramides are a group of polyphenolic compounds unique to oats that have been found to increase bone density by promoting calcium absorption and inhibiting the breakdown of collagen in bones. This property helps strengthen bone tissue and prevent fractures.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, present in herbal oat creams, have antioxidant properties that help protect bones from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can lead to bone loss and osteoporosis. This property helps preserve bone health and reduce the risk of age-related bone diseases.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have been shown to stimulate collagen production and improve skin elasticity, making them beneficial for bone health.
Stems: Containing a significant amount of silica, an essential mineral for bone formation and density, which can help promote strong and healthy bones.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh avena sativa leaves and dry them using shade drying or air circulation within 24 hours of harvesting.
Blend 20g dried avena sativa with 100ml carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the blend at low temperature for 30 minutes, then increase to moderate heat for an additional 1 hour and 15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Allow the infused oil to cool and solidify completely before whipping it with 10g beeswax and 5 drops lavender essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with bone health because they contain valerenic acid, a compound that has been shown to increase bone density and improve bone quality. The cream's active ingredients work by stimulating the production of osteoblasts, cells responsible for bone formation, while also inhibiting the activity of osteoclasts, which break down bone tissue. This dual action helps to promote strong, healthy bones, reducing the risk of conditions such as osteoporosis and fractures. Regular use of valerian cream may also help alleviate symptoms associated with joint pain and arthritis.
remedy_properties: Bone Mineralization: Valerian root has been shown to enhance bone mineralization by increasing calcium deposition, which can lead to stronger and denser bones.

Pain Relief: Valerian cream's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties can help relieve pain associated with conditions like arthritis, fibromyalgia, and lower back strain, which can be beneficial for bone health.

Bone Resorption Inhibition: Valerian root has been found to inhibit osteoclastic activity, which means it can help prevent excessive bone resorption (breakdown) that occurs with conditions like osteoporosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in valerenic acid, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, making them effective in soothing joint and bone issues.
Rhizomes: Valerian rhizomes contain isovaleric acid, which has been shown to improve calcium absorption and promote bone density, making them beneficial for bone health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots, wash them thoroughly with distilled water, and chop them into small pieces.
Combine one part chopped valeriana officinalis roots with three parts organic coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, allowing the roots to infuse their properties into the oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid.
Mix one part of the strained oil with two parts organic shea butter and one teaspoon beeswax in a double boiler, heating until melted and blended.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with bone health because it contains menthol, a compound that has been shown to stimulate osteoblasts, the cells responsible for building new bone tissue. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the body, which is known to contribute to conditions such as osteoporosis. By promoting healthy bone growth and reducing inflammation, herbal peppermint creams may help support strong bones and alleviate symptoms associated with bone disorders.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Antioxidants in peppermint creams, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to neutralize free radicals that can damage bones and lead to osteoporosis.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds, like menthol and menthone, reduce inflammation in the body, which is a major risk factor for bone loss and osteoporosis.

Calcium absorption enhancer: The volatile oils present in peppermint creams have been shown to enhance calcium absorption from food, leading to stronger bones and potentially reducing the risk of osteopenia and osteoporosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Peppermint roots are rich in calcium and magnesium, which are essential for bone density and strength.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of peppermint contain salicylic acid, a natural anti-inflammatory that helps reduce joint pain and inflammation associated with osteoporosis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality mentha x piperita leaves in the early morning or evening when they are at their most potent and fragrant.
Dry the fresh mint leaves using a food dehydrator set at 105°f for 6 hours or by air-drying them for 24 hours.
Combine 250 grams of dried mentha x piperita with 100 grams of beeswax and 200 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax melts completely and the mixture reaches a temperature of 180°f.
Remove the cream from the heat and let it cool to room temperature before transferring it to sterilized containers for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fenugreek creams helps with bone health because they contain saponins, which have been shown to inhibit osteoclast activity, thereby reducing bone resorption and promoting bone density. Additionally, fenugreek has been found to stimulate collagen synthesis, helping to repair and strengthen bones. By addressing these key mechanisms, herbal fenugreek creams can provide a natural and effective way to support healthy bone development and maintenance, potentially reducing the risk of osteoporosis and related conditions.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Fenugreek cream's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to bone degradation and osteoporosis, promoting overall bone health.

Calcium absorption enhancement: Fenugreek cream contains compounds that enhance calcium absorption from the gut, ensuring adequate calcium levels in the body, which is essential for maintaining strong bones.

Bone formation stimulation: Fenugreek cream's saponins have been shown to stimulate bone formation and inhibit bone resorption, leading to improved bone density and reduced risk of osteoporosis-related fractures.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the fenugreek plant contain a high concentration of saponins, which have been shown to promote bone density and reduce osteoporosis risk.
Seeds: Fenugreek seeds are rich in calcium, magnesium, and phosphorus, making them an effective ingredient in medicinal creams for bone health, as they can help strengthen and repair bones.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry trigonella foenum graecum leaves in shade for 2-3 weeks.
Grind 20g of dried leaves into fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Mix 10g of ground leaves with 30ml of coconut oil and 5g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60°c for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally to melt beeswax and infuse oil.
Strain the hot mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container and let it cool to room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Safflower creams helps with bone health because they contain a unique compound called safflower oleic acid, which has been shown to inhibit the activity of certain enzymes that break down bone tissue. This results in increased bone density and reduced risk of osteoporosis. Additionally, safflower creams are rich in antioxidants and fatty acids that help to nourish and support bone health. By promoting healthy bone function, safflower creams can help to alleviate symptoms such as joint pain and stiffness, leading to improved overall bone health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Omega-6 fatty acids: Omega-6 fatty acids, particularly linoleic acid, have anti-inflammatory effects and can help reduce joint pain and inflammation associated with osteoarthritis.
Vitamin E: Vitamin E, an antioxidant present in safflower oil, helps protect bones from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to bone loss and osteoporosis.

Fatty acid composition: The unique fatty acid composition of safflower oil, including high levels of linoleic acid and alpha-linolenic acid, has been shown to promote bone density and reduce the risk of fractures.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the safflower plant contain compounds that help promote bone growth and density, making them effective in treating osteoporosis.
Seeds: Safflower seeds are a rich source of omega-6 fatty acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce joint pain and inflammation associated with bone health issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry safflower flowers (carthamus tinctorius) in the morning, ensuring optimal potency and flavor retention.
Grind 20 grams of dried safflower flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine the powdered safflower with 30 grams of sweet almond oil in a double boiler, heating slowly over low heat for 15 minutes.
Steep the mixture at 160°f for 1 hour, then strain it through cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding solids and reserving infused oil.
Whisk in 10 grams of beeswax and 5 grams of coconut oil, allowing the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for 30 minutes before dispensing.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for bone health are topical preparations that utilize plant extracts to promote bone density, reduce inflammation, and alleviate pain associated with osteoporosis and other bone-related conditions. The best herbal creams for bone health include Glycyrrhiza glabra, Equisetum arvense, Curcuma longa, Zingiber officinale, Withania somnifera, Astragalus membranaceus, Panax ginseng, Vitis vinifera, Camellia sinensis, and Ginkgo biloba. If left untreated, bone health issues can lead to debilitating conditions, such as osteoporosis-induced fractures, chronic pain, and loss of mobility, drastically impacting daily life and independence.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to bone health that are treated with herbal creams are joint pain and inflammation, skin conditions such as eczema and acne, and even hair and nail problems. Herbal creams containing ingredients like arnica, aloe vera, and saw palmetto can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, psoriasis, and other skin irritations, while also promoting healthy hair and nail growth.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with bone health is a blend of Turmeric, Ginger, and Horsetail creams. Turmeric contains curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce joint pain and inflammation. Ginger has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that aid in pain relief and improve circulation. Horsetail, rich in silica, promotes bone growth and density. By combining these creams, individuals can enjoy a natural, holistic approach to maintaining bone health and alleviating symptoms associated with bone disorders. Regular application can lead to improved bone density and reduced risk of osteoporosis.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of family & reproductive health, herbal creams may be beneficial for bone health due to the significant increase in femoral neck Bone Mineral Density (BMD) found in the study. The study involved a comprehensive systematic search of the literature, analyzing 16 interventional studies with 968 participants, and found that the femoral neck BMD increased significantly (p=0.03) after treatment with certain herbal medicines. This suggests that herbal creams, particularly those containing curcumin, cinnamon, or other studied herbs, may help improve bone density and potentially reduce the risk of osteoporosis.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with bone health because they contain licorice root extract, which is rich in glycyrrhizin and flavonoids. These compounds have been shown to stimulate the production of osteocalcin, a protein essential for bone mineralization and density. Additionally, glycyrrhizin has been found to inhibit the activity of osteoclasts, cells responsible for bone resorption. By promoting osteoblast activity and inhibiting osteoclast activity, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams may help maintain bone health and reduce the risk of osteoporosis and related conditions.
remedy_constituents: Licoricidin: Acts as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, which helps reduce oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to bone degradation.
Glycyrrhizin: Exhibits osteoblastic activity, stimulating the formation of new bone tissue and promoting bone growth, which can help alleviate conditions such as osteoporosis.
Flavonoids: Possesses anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help protect bone cells from damage, promote bone mineralization, and support overall bone health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and promote bone health.
Leaves: The leaves are used to provide anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that support bone health and reduce the risk of bone-related diseases.
Roots (second mention): The roots are also used for their potential to support the absorption of calcium and other minerals essential for bone health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root and grind it into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine the powder with 100 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes or until it reaches a temperature of 160°f to 180°f.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool for 10 minutes then whip it using an electric mixer.
Transfer the whipped mixture to a glass jar and store it in a cool dry place for 24 hours before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Equisetum arvense creams helps with bone health because they are rich in flavonoids and caffeic acid, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can aid in reducing joint pain and inflammation. The creams also contain silica, a mineral that is essential for bone health and can help to strengthen bones and improve their density. Additionally, Equisetum arvense has been traditionally used to support bone health by promoting collagen production and enhancing bone mineralization, making it a popular natural remedy for bone-related issues.
remedy_constituents: Silica: Helps with bone health by promoting collagen synthesis, which is essential for the formation and maintenance of bone tissue.
Saponins: Contribute to bone health by potentially inhibiting osteoclast activity, thereby reducing bone resorption and promoting bone density.
Phenolic acids: May help with bone health by exhibiting antioxidant properties, which can reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in bone tissue, ultimately promoting bone health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Equisetum arvense are used to make creams for bone health because they contain silica and other minerals that help strengthen bones.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Equisetum arvense are used to make creams for bone health because they contain high amounts of silica, which is essential for bone development and strength.
Stems: The stems of Equisetum arvense are used to make creams for bone health because they contain silica and other minerals that help improve bone density and reduce the risk of fractures.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of dried equisetum arvense stems in early summer when it's in full bloom for 30 minutes.
Steep the dried equisetum arvense in 2 cups of boiling water for 5-7 minutes then let it infuse for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl and discard the solids for 20 minutes.
Mix 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a saucepan over low heat for 10 minutes.
Combine the infused equisetum arvense liquid with the beeswax and coconut oil mixture and whip until thickened for 10 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams helps with bone health because they contain curcumin, a natural compound with potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. This compound stimulates the production of osteoprotegerin, a protein that helps protect bones from damage and promotes bone density. Additionally, curcumin has been shown to increase the expression of genes involved in bone formation and reduce the activity of genes involved in bone resorption. As a result, Curcuma longa creams may help maintain strong bones and reduce the risk of osteoporosis and other bone-related disorders.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Helps with bone health by inhibiting the activity of enzymes that break down bone tissue, thus slowing down osteoclastic activity.
Demethoxycurcumin: Helps with bone health by promoting the differentiation and maturation of osteoblasts, which are cells responsible for bone formation.
Volatile oils (specifically, beta-pinene): Helps with bone health by reducing inflammation in the body, which can contribute to bone degradation and osteoporosis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Curcuma longa rhyzomes contain high amounts of curcumin, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and promote bone health.
Roots: Turmeric root extracts are used to create creams that support bone health by reducing joint pain and inflammation.
Stems: Although less commonly used, turmeric stem extracts have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate bone-related discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Grate 1 tablespoon of dried curcuma longa roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Combine 1 teaspoon of the powdered curcuma longa with 2 teaspoons of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes or until the beeswax melts completely.
Add 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 2 teaspoons of shea butter to the melted mixture and stir well.
Pour the cream into a clean glass jar and store it in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with bone health because of its anti-inflammatory properties and high antioxidant content. The active compound, gingerol, stimulates blood flow and helps to reduce inflammation in the joints, promoting healthier bones. Additionally, ginger has been shown to increase osteoclast inhibition, preventing bone breakdown and promoting bone density. By incorporating ginger creams into a bone health regimen, individuals may experience improved bone strength and reduced risk of osteoporosis and related conditions. This makes Zingiber officinale creams a valuable addition to bone health routines.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Help with bone health by reducing inflammation and pain associated with bone disorders, promoting relaxation and reducing muscle spasms.
Zingiberene: Contribute to bone health by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and reducing oxidative stress, which can damage bone tissue.
6-Shogaol: Assist in bone health by increasing bone formation and reducing bone resorption, thereby improving bone density and reducing the risk of osteoporosis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are commonly used for their high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce bone pain and inflammation.
Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale contain a compound called gingerol, which has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help protect bone health.
Stems: The stems of the plant contain a compound called sesquiterpene, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce bone pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Extract 1 cup of zingiber officinale rhizomes and chop into fine pieces with a knife.
Combine the chopped zingiber officinale with 2 cups of distilled water in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture at 100 degrees celsius for 30 minutes or until the liquid reduces.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Mix the strained liquid with 1 cup of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil.

remedies.remedy_desc: Withania somnifera creams helps with bone health because they contain bioactive compounds that promote osteoblast activity, leading to increased bone formation. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the joints, which can contribute to bone damage. Additionally, Withania somnifera is known to enhance the expression of genes involved in bone metabolism, resulting in improved bone density and reduced risk of osteoporosis. This natural approach supports overall bone health and may help alleviate symptoms of bone-related disorders.
remedy_constituents: Withanolides: These compounds help with bone health by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and reducing oxidative stress, which can contribute to bone degradation.

Withaferin A: This constituent helps with bone health by suppressing the activity of osteoclasts, which are cells responsible for bone resorption, thereby promoting bone density and reducing the risk of osteoporosis.

Withanoside IV: This compound helps with bone health by promoting the expression of osteoblasts, which are cells responsible for bone formation, and inhibiting the production of inflammatory mediators that can disrupt bone metabolism.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in bioactive compounds like withanolides, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help promote bone health.
Leaves: The leaves contain withanolides and other beneficial compounds that have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote bone growth.
Seeds: The seeds are a rich source of withanolides and other phytochemicals that have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, supporting bone health.
remedy_recipe: Extract 30-40 grams of dried withania somnifera roots in 500 milliliters of water and boil for 30-40 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and collect the liquid extract discard the solids.
Mix the extract with 10 grams of beeswax and 5 grams of candelilla wax in a double boiler.
Add 10 milliliters of jojoba oil and 5 milliliters of vitamin e oil to the mixture and heat gently.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus creams helps with bone health because it is rich in antioxidants and polysaccharides that stimulate collagen production, promoting healthy bone matrix and density. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to bone loss. Additionally, Astragalus membranaceus has been shown to enhance bone formation by regulating osteoblast activity, cells responsible for bone growth and repair. This results in stronger, more resilient bones.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins help with bone health by promoting bone formation and inhibiting bone resorption, thereby reducing the risk of osteoporosis and fractures.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids help with bone health by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines that can lead to bone degradation, and promoting the activity of osteoblasts, which are responsible for bone formation.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids help with bone health by stimulating the activity of osteoblasts, inhibiting the activity of osteoclasts, and reducing oxidative stress, which can contribute to bone loss and osteoporosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain astragalosides, a group of compounds that have been shown to promote bone health and prevent osteoporosis.
Rhyzomes: They are rich in astragalosides and other bioactive compounds that help to stimulate bone growth and density.
Barks: They contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to protect bone health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of astragalus membranaceus roots and clean them thoroughly under cold running water for 10 minutes.
Dry the roots in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 4 hours to remove excess moisture completely.
Grind 20g of dried astragalus membranaceus roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 10g of ground astragalus membranaceus powder with 20g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 70°c for 30 minutes and then pour it into jars for storage purposes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng creams helps with bone health because it contains bioactive compounds that stimulate bone growth and density. These compounds, including ginsenosides, have been shown to increase osteoblast activity, promoting new bone formation. Additionally, ginseng creams may enhance bone mineralization by regulating the balance of calcium and phosphorus in the body. This can lead to improved bone strength, reduced risk of osteoporosis, and enhanced overall bone health. Regular use may also support joint health and mobility.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These steroid saponins help with bone health by inhibiting the production of osteoclasts, which are cells responsible for bone resorption, and promoting osteoblasts, which are cells responsible for bone formation.
Ginsenolic acid: This phenolic compound helps with bone health by inhibiting the activity of osteoclasts and promoting the activity of osteoblasts, leading to an overall increase in bone density.

Panaxynol: This lignan phenol helps with bone health by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which can lead to bone loss, and promoting the production of anti-inflammatory cytokines, which can help maintain bone health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in ginsenosides, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties beneficial for bone health.
Leaves: Contain ginsenosides and other bioactive compounds that help to improve bone density and reduce inflammation.
Barks: Known to contain ginsenosides and other compounds that promote bone growth, density, and strength.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 panax ginseng roots, clean and peel them, and then dry them in a shaded area for 2 days.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 1 tablespoon of the powder with 1 tablespoon of beeswax in a double boiler, heat until melted for 10 minutes.
Combine the melted mixture with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of shea butter, stir until smooth for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into small containers, let it cool and solidify for 2 hours before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitis vinifera creams helps with bone health because they contain resveratrol, a polyphenol compound extracted from the grapevine. Resveratrol has been shown to stimulate bone growth, reduce bone resorption, and improve bone density. This results in stronger bones, reduced risk of osteoporosis, and a lower likelihood of fractures. The anti-inflammatory properties of resveratrol also help to protect bone cells from damage, promoting overall bone health and well-being. Regular application of Vitis vinifera creams may support bone health and maintenance.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: These compounds help with bone health by exhibiting antioxidant properties that reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to bone loss and osteoporosis.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Vitis vinifera creams help with bone health by modulating the activity of osteoclasts, which are cells responsible for bone resorption, thereby promoting bone density.

Tannins: Tannins in Vitis vinifera creams help with bone health by inhibiting the activity of osteoclasts, reducing bone resorption, and promoting bone formation, which ultimately leads to improved bone health.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are rich in antioxidants, polyphenols, and saponins that help promote bone health and reduce inflammation.
Fruits: The extract from the fruits of Vitis vinifera, also known as grape seed extract, is high in resveratrol which is beneficial for bone health.
Leaves: The leaves of the Vitis vinifera plant contain compounds like flavonoids and polyphenols that have been shown to promote bone density and reduce osteoporosis risk.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried vitis vinifera flowers at peak blooming time for maximum potency retention.
Combine 50 grams of dried vitis vinifera flowers with 500 milliliters of carrier oil in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 2 hours while stirring occasionally to ensure even infusion.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container to remove solids.
Add 10 milliliters of vitamin e oil and 5 milliliters of essential oil to the infused oil mixture and stir well.

remedies.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis creams helps with bone health because they are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that promote bone density and strength. The catechins present in Camellia sinensis have been shown to inhibit the activity of osteoclasts, the cells responsible for bone breakdown, thereby helping to maintain bone health and prevent conditions such as osteoporosis. Regular use of Camellia sinensis creams may also improve bone mineralization and reduce the risk of fractures.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help with bone health by promoting bone formation and reducing the activity of osteoclasts, which break down bone tissue.

Catechins: Catechins, particularly epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), have been shown to prevent bone loss by suppressing the activity of osteoclasts and promoting the activity of osteoblasts, which build bone tissue.

Theaflavins: Theaflavins have been found to have antioxidant properties, which help protect bone cells from oxidative stress and promote bone health by reducing inflammation and enhancing bone formation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: High in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help protect bones from oxidative stress and promote bone health.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and other compounds that may help reduce inflammation and promote bone growth.
Seeds: Contain squalene, a compound with potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may aid in bone health.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 tablespoon of camellia sinensis leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the camellia sinensis infusion through a cheesecloth or fine mesh into a clean bowl.
Combine 2 ounces of the camellia sinensis infusion with 1 ounce of distilled water and 1 teaspoon of beeswax.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water for 10 minutes.
Allow the camellia sinensis cream to cool and thicken at room temperature for 30-40 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams helps with bone health because it increases blood flow to the bones, promoting better calcium absorption and reducing inflammation. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in Ginkgo biloba also have antioxidant properties, which help to protect bone cells from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba's ability to improve circulation may aid in the delivery of essential nutrients to the bones, supporting overall bone health and potentially reducing the risk of osteoporosis and other bone-related disorders.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help with bone health by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in bones, which can contribute to bone degeneration.
Terpenoids: Have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce bone resorption and promote bone density.
Bilobalide: Act as a neuroprotective agent and may help regulate the activity of osteoclasts, cells responsible for bone resorption.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Ginkgo biloba leaves are the most commonly used part for creams due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to promote bone health and reduce inflammation.
Barks: Ginkgo biloba barks are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect bones from damage and promote healthy bone growth.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds contain flavonoids and other compounds that may help to stimulate bone growth and reduce bone loss, making them a useful ingredient in creams for bone health.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 20 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 2 minutes.
Combine the ginkgo biloba powder with 50 grams of coconut oil and 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler over low heat for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally to prevent burning.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool for 10 minutes before whipping it with a hand mixer for 5 minutes.
Pour the cooled mixture into a glass jar and store it in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.
